1.description: OOT purchase share New York. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Earnings, net worth, and homeownership across age and income groups. The left panels correspond to the data and based on the Survey of Consumer Finances (all 1983 to 2010 waves). All survey data are first expressed in 2010 real dollars before averaging across survey waves. The right panels correspond to the benchmark model. The top row depicts household earnings. The middle row depicts household wealth. The bottom row depicts the homeownership rate. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Welfare effects of increase in OOT housing demand. The figure presents the consumption-equivalent welfare change from an increase in OOT housing demand in the baseline model. All numbers are in percentage points, and thus, −5.0 for a group of households means that the average household in that group would need to receive 5.0% of its average consumption to be as well off after the OOT increase as before. All panels split households into 20 age groups of four years. The top panel splits households by tenure status, the middle panel by income (including retirement income), and the bottom panel by wealth (financial wealth, positive or negative, plus housing wealth). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Redistributing OOT tax revenue to low-income households. Panel A plots the effect of OOT inflow on welfare by productivity group in the baseline model. Panel B plots the same statistic for an alternative model in which OOT buyers are charged an extra 3.46% property tax on top of the baseline property tax rate of 6.3%. In Panel C, we report the same statistic when the extra tax revenue from the OOT property tax surcharge is redistributed to the low-productivity households. In Panel D, all of the tax revenue raised from the OOT buyers is redistributed to the low-productivity households. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Labor income calibration. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
